question: 현재 UBOB에서 가장 인기 있는 학습 과정들이 뭐야? 지금 가장 뜨거운 1위 과정의 상세 정보 — 목차, 수료 조건, 학습 현황까지 — 같이 알려줘.

server_info.name: UBOB Pro MCP Server
server_info.author: @sigco3111/ubob_pro_mcp
server_info.overview: UBOB Pro MCP Server는 학습자가 유밥 학습 플랫폼을 개인화된 방식으로 활용할 수 있는 파이썬 기반 백엔드 중심의 도구입니다. 이 서버는 Claude 데스크탑 앱, Cursor AI, 그리고 향후 ChatGPT 데스크탑 앱과 같은 다양한 AI 에이전트와 연동되어 사용자가 자연어 인터페이스를 통해 학습 데이터에 접근하고 활용할 수 있도록 지원합니다. 복잡한 대시보드 없이 사용자는 AI와의 대화를 통해 학습 콘텐츠와 데이터를 직관적으로 탐색하고 활용할 수 있습니다. 이 서버는 Smithery.ai 플랫폼을 통해 배포되어 다양한 AI 에이전트와 쉽게 연동될 수 있습니다.
server_info.python_sdk:
import mcp
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
import json
import base64

smithery_api_key = ""
url = f"https://server.smithery.ai/@sigco3111/ubob_pro_mcp/mcp?api_key={smithery_api_key}"

async def main():
    # Connect to the server using HTTP client
    async with streamablehttp_client(url) as (read_stream, write_stream, _):
        async with mcp.ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

tools.name: ubob_login
tools.description:
UBOB Pro 서비스에 로그인합니다.

    명령줄 인자로 제공된 인증 정보를 사용하여 UBOB Pro 서비스에 로그인합니다.
    인증 정보가 명령줄 인자에서 제공되지 않은 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.

    Returns:
        dict: 로그인 결과 및 토큰 정보를 포함하는 딕셔너리

tools.name: login_with_credentials
tools.description:
사용자가 제공한 인증 정보로 로그인합니다.
    
    사용자가 직접 입력한 사용자명, 비밀번호, 앱 ID로 UBOB Pro 서비스에 로그인합니다.
    입력 형식은 다음과 같습니다:
    - '사용자명: YOUR_USERNAME
비밀번호: YOUR_PASSWORD
앱 ID: YOUR_APP_ID' 또는
    - 'YOUR_USERNAME, YOUR_PASSWORD, APP_ID'
    
    Args:
        credentials: 사용자가 입력한 인증 정보 문자열
        
    Returns:
        로그인 결과 및 토큰 정보를 포함하는 딕셔너리

tools.name: check_login_status
tools.description: 현재 로그인 상태를 확인합니다.

tools.name: get_study_info
tools.description:
사용자의 학습 정보를 조회합니다.

    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 현재 로그인된 사용자의 학습 정보를 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.

    Returns:
        dict: 학습 정보를 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_study_analysis
tools.description:
학습 데이터를 분석하여 인사이트를 제공합니다.

    사용자의 학습 데이터를 분석하여 학습 패턴, 추천 학습 시간, 
    카테고리별 학습 비율 등 유용한 인사이트를 제공합니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.

    Returns:
        학습 분석 결과를 포함하는 딕셔너리

tools.name: get_myroom_info
tools.description:
내 강의실 정보를 가져옵니다.

    UBOB Pro 서비스에 로그인하여 사용자의 내 강의실 정보를 조회합니다.
    내 강의실에는 학습 신청 과정, 수료 과정, 학습 중인 과정 등의 정보가 포함됩니다.
    2023년 1월 1일부터 12월 31일까지의 데이터를 조회합니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.

    Returns:
        dict: 내 강의실 정보를 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_recent_searches
tools.description:
최근 검색 이력을 조회합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 현재 로그인된 사용자의 최근 검색 이력을 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Returns:
        dict: 최근 검색 이력을 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_popular_searches
tools.description:
인기 검색어를 조회합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 인기 검색어 목록을 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Returns:
        dict: 인기 검색어 목록을 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: search_content
tools.description:
검색을 수행합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 검색을 수행합니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Args:
        search_text: 검색어
        mode: 검색 종류 (ALL, SERIES, AUTHOR, SNACK, REFERENCE)
        skip: 시작 위치
        take: 검색 개수
        sort_type: 정렬 방식 (NEW, POPUL, NAME)
    
    Returns:
        dict: 검색 결과를 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_series_detail
tools.description:
과정 상세 정보를 조회합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 특정 과정의 상세 정보를 가져옵니다.
    과정의 콘텐츠, 목차, 수료 조건, 학습 현황 등 상세 정보를 제공합니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Args:
        series_id: 과정의 ID (OriId가 아닌 일반 Id)
        order_id: 학습신청 ID (선택 사항)
    
    Returns:
        dict: 과정 상세 정보를 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: generate_series_link
tools.description:
과정 링크를 생성합니다.
    
    로그인 시 받은 appUrl 값과 과정 ID를 결합하여 과정 상세 페이지 링크를 생성합니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Args:
        series_id: 과정의 ID
        validate: 과정 ID 유효성 검사 여부 (선택 사항)
    
    Returns:
        dict: 생성된 링크 정보를 포함하는 결과

tools.name: get_favorlist_info
tools.description:
최근 학습 목록을 조회합니다.

    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 현재 로그인된 사용자의 최근 학습 목록을 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.

    Returns:
        dict: 최근 학습 목록을 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_series_for_period
tools.description:
특정 기간의 신규과정 리스트를 조회합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 특정 기간동안 신규과정으로 인식된 과정 목록을 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Args:
        start_date: 검색 시작일 (yyyy-MM-dd 형식)
        end_date: 검색 종료일 (yyyy-MM-dd 형식)
    
    Returns:
        dict: 특정 기간의 신규과정 리스트를 포함하는 JSON 응답 또는 오류 메시지

tools.name: get_series_ranking
tools.description:
인기과정 랭킹 정보를 조회합니다.
    
    UBOB Pro 서비스의 API를 호출하여 다양한 타입의 인기과정 랭킹 정보를 가져옵니다.
    로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인을 시도합니다.
    인증 정보가 없는 경우 사용자에게 직접 입력을 요청합니다.
    
    Args:
        list_type: 리스트 타입 (NEW50, TOP50YEAR, TOP50MONTH, TOP50NOW, TOP50ALL, TOP50WEEK)
        option1: 옵션1 (리스트 타입에 따라 필요한 값이 다름)
        option2: 옵션2 (리스트 타입에 따라 필요한 값이 다름)
        option3: 옵션3 (리스트 타입에 따라 필요한 값이 다름)
        skip: 건너뛸 항목 수 (기본값: 0)
        take: 가져올 항목 수 (기본값: 50)
    
    Returns:
        dict: 인기과정 랭킹 정보를 포함하는 딕셔너리

labels.analysis: The UBOB Pro MCP Server is designed to enhance personalized learning experiences through a Python-based backend system. It serves as an intermediary between AI agents like Claude, Cursor AI, and future AI tools, allowing users to interact with educational content using natural language. The server provides various tools for accessing learning data, analyzing study patterns, retrieving course information, and performing searches within the UBOB learning platform. Its core functionality revolves around education, data retrieval, and user-specific content discovery.
labels.reasoning: The primary label "Education" was chosen because the server's primary functionality is centered around learning and educational content management. Secondary label "Data Analysis & Processing" was selected due to tools like "get_study_analysis" that provide insights from learning data. "API Integration" is also relevant as the server heavily relies on API calls to interact with the UBOB platform. No custom label is needed as the predefined categories adequately cover its functionality.

server_analysis: UBOB Pro MCP Server는 학습 플랫폼 UBOB Pro의 데이터에 접근하고 활용할 수 있는 14가지 도구를 제공합니다. 주요 기능은 크게 세 가지 범주로 나뉩니다: (1) 인증 관련 도구(로그인, 상태 확인), (2) 학습 데이터 조회 도구(학습 정보, 강의실 정보, 최근 학습 목록, 검색 이력 등), (3) 과정 탐색 도구(검색, 인기 과정 랭킹, 과정 상세 정보, 기간별 신규 과정 등). 이 서버는 로그인 상태를 자동 관리하며, 로그인되지 않은 경우 자동으로 로그인 시도를 하는 특징이 있습니다.

question: I'm planning to move some USDC from Ethereum to Arbitrum, but before I commit, I want to make sure both chains are actually supported by LiFi's bridging network. Can you first check that both Ethereum and Arbitrum are in the list of supported chains, and then pull the current bridge rates for USDC from Ethereum to Arbitrum so I can compare the best route?

server_info.name: Bridge Rates Server
server_info.author: @kukapay/bridge-rates-mcp
server_info.overview: Provide real-time cross-chain bridge rates and optimal transfer routes to support decision-making by onchain AI agents. Retrieve detailed bridge rates, supported chains, and bridges in easy-to-read Markdown tables. Enhance your cross-chain transfer insights with up-to-date data from LI.FI.
server_info.python_sdk:
import mcp
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
import json
import base64

smithery_api_key = ""
url = f"https://server.smithery.ai/@kukapay/bridge-rates-mcp/mcp?api_key={smithery_api_key}"

async def main():
    # Connect to the server using HTTP client
    async with streamablehttp_client(url) as (read_stream, write_stream, _):
        async with mcp.ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

tools.name: getBridgeRates
tools.description: Fetches the cross-chain bridge rate for a token pair between two chains

fromAmount.description: Amount to bridge (in smallest token unit, default: 10000000)

tools.name: getSupportedChains
tools.description: Fetches the list of chains supported by LiFi for cross-chain bridging

tools.name: getSupportedBridges
tools.description: Fetches the list of bridges supported by LiFi for cross-chain bridging

labels.analysis: The MCP Server is designed to provide real-time data and analytics for cross-chain bridge operations, specifically focusing on bridge rates, supported chains, and supported bridges. It leverages LiFi's infrastructure to offer actionable insights for AI agents and users looking to optimize cross-chain transfers. The tools provided by this server primarily cater to the cryptocurrency and blockchain domain, with a strong emphasis on facilitating informed decision-making for cross-chain transactions.
labels.reasoning: The primary label "Cryptocurrency & Blockchain" was chosen because the server's core functionality revolves around cross-chain bridging, a key operation within the blockchain ecosystem. "Data Analysis & Processing" is included as a secondary label due to the server's focus on providing up-to-date, structured data for analysis. No custom label is needed as the predefined categories adequately cover the server's functionality.

server_analysis: The Bridge Rates Server provides three tools for cross-chain bridge analysis. `getBridgeRates` retrieves real-time bridge rates for a specific token pair between two chains. `getSupportedChains` returns all blockchain networks supported by LiFi for bridging. `getSupportedBridges` lists all bridge protocols available through LiFi. Together, these tools enable users to explore the cross-chain landscape, verify chain/bridge availability, and obtain current transfer rates.

question: I'm planning a virtual museum tour focused on Renaissance art. Can you suggest some artworks that match this interest? Once you've given me a few recommendations, I'd like to dive deeper into one of them — I want to know its full description, provenance history, and technical details like medium and dimensions.

server_info.name: Cultural Heritage Server
server_info.author: @RabiaDogan41/mcp
server_info.overview: Provide access to European cultural heritage collections and artworks. Search, browse, and get detailed information about famous artworks from major European museums. Receive AI-powered personalized cultural recommendations based on user interests.
server_info.python_sdk:
import mcp
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
import json
import base64

smithery_api_key = ""
url = f"https://server.smithery.ai/@RabiaDogan41/mcp/mcp?api_key={smithery_api_key}"

async def main():
    # Connect to the server using HTTP client
    async with streamablehttp_client(url) as (read_stream, write_stream, _):
        async with mcp.ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

tools.name: search_cultural_heritage
tools.description:
Search for cultural heritage items (artworks, artifacts, etc.) from European institutions.
    
    Args:
        query: Search term (artist name, artwork title, type, etc.)
        limit: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)
    
    Returns:
        Formatted list of cultural heritage items with details

tools.name: get_artwork_details
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a specific artwork or cultural item.
    
    Args:
        artwork_id: Unique identifier of the artwork
    
    Returns:
        Detailed information about the artwork including description, provenance, and technical details

tools.name: get_collections_by_institution
tools.description:
Get artworks and cultural items from a specific museum or cultural institution.
    
    Args:
        institution_name: Name of the cultural institution (e.g., "Louvre", "British Museum")
    
    Returns:
        List of cultural items from the specified institution

tools.name: get_cultural_recommendations
tools.description:
Get AI-powered recommendations for cultural heritage items based on user interests.
    
    Args:
        user_interest: User's cultural interests (e.g., "modern art", "renaissance", "impressionist")
    
    Returns:
        Personalized recommendations for cultural heritage exploration

labels.analysis: The MCP Server is dedicated to accessing and exploring European cultural heritage collections, artworks, and recommendations. Its core functionality revolves around searching, retrieving detailed information about cultural items, and providing AI-powered personalized suggestions based on user interests. The tools focus on artwork research, institutional browsing, and curation assisted by AI.
labels.reasoning: The primary label "Web Search & Research" was chosen because the server's primary function is to search, retrieve, and provide detailed information about cultural heritage items—akin to specialized research tools. The secondary label "AI/ML Tools" was added due to the AI-powered recommendation system, while "Education" also applies given the server's role in facilitating cultural learning.

server_analysis: The Cultural Heritage Server provides four tools for exploring European cultural heritage: (1) search_cultural_heritage allows searching artworks and artifacts by keyword, (2) get_artwork_details retrieves in-depth information about a specific artwork by its ID, (3) get_collections_by_institution lists items from a particular museum or cultural institution, and (4) get_cultural_recommendations delivers AI-powered personalized suggestions based on user interests. These tools work well together in workflows where a user first discovers items broadly (via search, institution browsing, or recommendations) and then dives deeper into a specific piece.

question: I'm working on improving the performance of our marketing landing page. Can you navigate to https://example.com and then show me all the network requests that are made when the page loads? I need to see which third-party resources are being pulled in and whether any of them are blocking the initial render.

server_info.name: Playwright Browser Automation Server
server_info.author: @JOBKO-QA/playwright-mcp-private
server_info.overview: Enable LLMs to interact with web pages through structured accessibility snapshots for fast, reliable browser automation without relying on vision models. Perform actions like clicking, typing, navigation, and file uploads deterministically using Playwright's accessibility tree. Supports both snapshot and vision modes, persistent or isolated profiles, and integrates seamlessly with popular MCP clients.
server_info.python_sdk:
import mcp
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
import json
import base64

smithery_api_key = ""
url = f"https://server.smithery.ai/@JOBKO-QA/playwright-mcp-private/mcp?api_key={smithery_api_key}"

async def main():
    # Connect to the server using HTTP client
    async with streamablehttp_client(url) as (read_stream, write_stream, _):
        async with mcp.ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

tools.name: browser_resize

tools.name: browser_console_messages

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be a single file or multiple files.

tools.name: browser_install
tools.description: Install the browser specified in the config. Call this if you get an error about the browser not being installed.

annotations.title: Install the browser specified in the config

tools.name: browser_press_key
tools.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: Go back to the previous page

tools.name: browser_navigate_forward
tools.description: Go forward to the next page

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: Returns all network requests since loading the page

tools.name: browser_pdf_save
tools.description: Save page as PDF

filename.description: File name to save the pdf to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.pdf` if not specified.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

raw.description: Whether to return without compression (in PNG format). Default is false, which returns a JPEG image.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to screenshot the element. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If element is provided, ref must be provided too.

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If ref is provided, element must be provided too.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startRef.description: Exact source element reference from the page snapshot

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endRef.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

tools.name: browser_tab_list
tools.description: List browser tabs

tools.name: browser_tab_new
tools.description: Open a new tab

url.description: The URL to navigate to in the new tab. If not provided, the new tab will be blank.

tools.name: browser_tab_select
tools.description: Select a tab by index

index.description: The index of the tab to select

tools.name: browser_tab_close
tools.description: Close a tab

index.description: The index of the tab to close. Closes current tab if not provided.

tools.name: browser_generate_playwright_test
tools.description: Generate a Playwright test for given scenario

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

labels.analysis: The MCP Server is specialized in web browser automation using Playwright's accessibility tree for deterministic interactions. It allows LLMs to perform actions like clicking, typing, navigating, and uploading files without relying on vision models. The tools focus on controlled browser interactions, page manipulation, and snapshot-based automation, making it highly reliable for web-based tasks.
labels.reasoning: The primary functionality of determistic web interactions aligns perfectly with the "Browser Automation" category. Given its focus on accessibility snapshots and structured interactions, it also fits well with "Web Search & Research" as a secondary label, as it can be used to automate information gathering. The toolset's versatility in browser control may also benefit "Development Tools" for QA/testing scenarios.

server_analysis: The Playwright Browser Automation Server provides a comprehensive set of tools for browser interaction. Its capabilities include: (1) Navigation tools for moving between URLs and managing tabs, (2) Interaction tools for clicking, typing, dragging, hovering, and selecting options, (3) Capture tools for taking screenshots, accessibility snapshots, and saving PDFs, (4) Diagnostic tools for inspecting console messages and network requests, (5) Utility tools for handling dialogs, waiting, resizing, and generating tests. The tools are designed to work in sequences, enabling complex browser automation workflows.

question: I'm setting up a Node.js project and need to understand the configuration for a third-party service. Can you grab the JSON configuration template from their public GitHub repository and also fetch the Markdown README that documents all the available settings? I want to compare the actual config structure with the documentation to make sure I'm not missing any important fields.

server_info.name: Fetch Server
server_info.author: @goswamig/fetch-mcp
server_info.overview: Fetch web content in various formats like HTML, JSON, plain text, and Markdown. Easily retrieve and transform online data with custom headers for your applications. Streamline your content fetching process with a simple and efficient server setup.
server_info.python_sdk:
import mcp
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
import json
import base64

smithery_api_key = ""
url = f"https://server.smithery.ai/@goswamig/fetch-mcp/mcp?api_key={smithery_api_key}"

async def main():
    # Connect to the server using HTTP client
    async with streamablehttp_client(url) as (read_stream, write_stream, _):
        async with mcp.ClientSession(read_stream, write_stream) as session:
            # Initialize the connection
            await session.initialize()
            # List available tools
            tools_result = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {', '.join([t.name for t in tools_result.tools])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(main())

tools.name: fetch_html
tools.description: Fetch a website and return the content as HTML

url.description: URL of the website to fetch

headers.description: Optional headers to include in the request

tools.name: fetch_markdown
tools.description: Fetch a website and return the content as Markdown

url.description: URL of the website to fetch

headers.description: Optional headers to include in the request

tools.name: fetch_txt
tools.description: Fetch a website, return the content as plain text (no HTML)

url.description: URL of the website to fetch

headers.description: Optional headers to include in the request

tools.name: fetch_json
tools.description: Fetch a JSON file from a URL

url.description: URL of the JSON to fetch

headers.description: Optional headers to include in the request

labels.analysis: The MCP Server "Fetch Server" is primarily designed to retrieve and transform web content in various formats including HTML, JSON, plain text, and Markdown. It provides a set of tools to fetch data from URLs, allowing users to specify the desired output format, which can be useful for web scraping, data extraction, and API interactions. The ability to customize headers makes it versatile for different use cases, such as automated data collection or application integration.
labels.reasoning: The primary label "Web Search & Research" is chosen because the server's core functionality revolves around fetching and transforming web content, which is a common operation in research and information gathering. The secondary label "Browser Automation" is selected due to its potential use in scraping and automated interactions with web content. A custom label "Content Extraction" is also included to highlight its specific role in retrieving formatted data from the web.

server_analysis: The Fetch Server provides four tools for retrieving web content in different formats: `fetch_html` returns raw HTML, `fetch_markdown` returns content as Markdown, `fetch_txt` returns plain text without HTML tags, and `fetch_json` fetches JSON files from URLs. These tools complement each other well for tasks that require comparing different representations of the same content, or combining structured data (JSON) with descriptive documentation (Markdown/HTML).
